title: Assembly Line Captains
statement_md:
Morning assembly. The whole class stands in one line on the ground, and you can see everyone's height from left to right.

The PT sir wants to pick **line captains**. A student is a line captain if they are **strictly taller** than every student standing to their right. The last student in the line has nobody on their right, so they are always a captain.

Given the heights in left-to-right order, print the heights of all the line captains, keeping the same left-to-right order.
input_format_md:
The first line contains a single integer `T` — the number of test cases.

For each test case:
- the first line contains a single integer `N` — the number of students in the line
- the second line contains `N` space-separated integers — the heights, left to right
output_format_md: For each test case, print one line containing the heights of all line captains from left to right, separated by single spaces. (There is always at least one captain — the last student.)

starter_code.python:
import sys

def solve_case(n, heights):
    # your code here — return the captain heights left to right, e.g. "17 5 2"
    return ""

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    pos = 0
    t = int(data[pos]); pos += 1
    for _ in range(t):
        n = int(data[pos]); pos += 1
        heights = [int(x) for x in data[pos:pos + n]]; pos += n
        print(solve_case(n, heights))

main()

reference_solution.python:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    pos = 0
    t = int(data[pos]); pos += 1
    out = []
    for _ in range(t):
        n = int(data[pos]); pos += 1
        heights = [int(x) for x in data[pos:pos + n]]; pos += n
        caps = []
        best = -1
        for h in reversed(heights):
            if h > best:
                caps.append(h)
                best = h
        caps.reverse()
        out.append(" ".join(map(str, caps)))
    sys.stdout.write("\n".join(out) + "\n")

main()

hints: A captain only cares about one thing: the tallest student somewhere on their right. Do you really need to re-check every student each time?
Walk the line from the right end, carrying the tallest height seen so far. A student strictly taller than that running maximum is a captain — collect them and reverse the list before printing.
